ᶄ Accounting Standard for Lease Transactions
Finance leases which do not transfer titles to lessees were accounted for as operating leases in the previous periods.
The TSE Group and its subsidiaries adopted “Accounting Standard for Lease Transactions” and “Guidance on

from this period. The depreciation of the leased assets of finance leases which do not transfer titles to lessees is
computed using the straight-line method based on the initial lease term with no estimated residual value. The

Operating Revenue Operating Income Net Income*
* Net income attributable to owners of the parent company.
• Increases in both trading services and clearing services revenue resulted in a 7.7% year-on-year increase in
operating revenue.
• While operating expenses increased, this was more than offset by an increase in operating revenue, resulting in
an 8.3% year-on-year increase in operating income and an 8.0% year-on-year increase in net income*.
• Both trading services and clearing services revenue increased due to an increase in trading of cash equities.
• In FY2025, the breakdown of operating revenue was revised to six categories with the addition of “System
Services Revenue.” For details, refer to “Revision of Operating Revenue Breakdown” (page12).
